questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by a chord at the center is twice the angle subtended at the circumference on the same side.
questions.answer: Using isosceles triangles formed by the radii and chord, central angle = 2 × inscribed angle.
answer_outline: Conclude central angle = 2 × angle at circumference

questions.question: Prove that the perpendicular from the center to a chord bisects the chord and the converse.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by radii and perpendicular are congruent; conversely, if a perpendicular bisects a chord, it passes through the center.
answer_outline: Draw chord AB and perpendicular OM
Conversely: draw bisector, show it passes through center

questions.question: Prove that equal chords of a circle are equidistant from the center and conversely.
questions.answer: Use congruent triangles formed by radii and perpendiculars from center to chords.
answer_outline: Draw equal chords AB and CD
Conversely: equal distances imply equal chords

questions.question: Prove that angles in the same segment of a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Use isosceles triangles formed by radii to chord endpoints and properties of inscribed angles.
answer_outline: Mark points C and D in same segment
Join radii to C and D

questions.question: Prove that opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral sum to 180°.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem to show sum of opposite angles equals 180°.
answer_outline: Identify opposite angles A and C

questions.question: Prove the tangent-chord angle theorem: angle between tangent and chord equals angle in alternate segment.
questions.answer: Construct triangle using radius and tangent to show equality of angles.
answer_outline: Draw tangent at point A and chord AB
Identify angle between tangent and chord
Show angle = angle in alternate segment

questions.question: Prove that the lengths of tangents drawn from an external point to a circle are equal.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by radii and tangents are congruent, so tangent lengths are equal.
answer_outline: Draw tangents PA and PB from external point P

questions.question: Prove that the radius drawn to the point of contact of a tangent is perpendicular to the tangent.
questions.answer: Use the property that radius is the shortest distance from center to line.
answer_outline: Draw radius OA to point of contact

questions.question: Prove that the product of segments of two intersecting chords is equal.
questions.answer: Triangles formed by intersecting chords are similar; hence products of segments are equal.
answer_outline: Draw intersecting chords AB and CD at P
Identify segments PA, PB, PC, PD

questions.question: Prove that the line joining centers of two externally touching circles passes through the point of contact.
questions.answer: Distance between centers equals sum of radii, so line joining centers passes through contact point.
answer_outline: Draw externally touching circles with centers O1 and O2

questions.question: Prove that the angle between two tangents drawn from an external point equals the difference of the angles subtended by lines joining the point to points of contact at the center.
questions.answer: Construct triangle using center and points of contact and use central angle properties.
answer_outline: Draw tangents PA and PB from external point P
Join center O to points of contact A and B

questions.question: Prove that if a line divides a chord (not passing through center) internally and externally in the same ratio, it passes through the center.
questions.answer: Use similar triangles formed with radii to show line passes through center.
answer_outline: Draw line dividing chord internally and externally

questions.question: Prove that the sum of opposite angles of a quadrilateral inscribed in a circle is 180° using the angle subtended by chords.
questions.answer: Use inscribed angle theorem for chords forming quadrilateral to show sum = 180°.
answer_outline: Use angle subtended by chord property

questions.question: Prove that the tangents at the ends of a diameter are parallel using properties of radii.
questions.answer: Radii are perpendicular to tangents, hence tangents at endpoints of diameter are parallel.
answer_outline: Draw tangents at A and B

questions.question: Prove that the angle subtended by a chord at the center is greater than that at any point on the remaining part of the circle.
questions.answer: Form triangle with radius and chord, show central angle > inscribed angle using triangle properties.
answer_outline: Mark center O and point C on remaining circle

questions.question: Prove that two tangents drawn from an external point form equal angles with the line joining the point to the center.
questions.answer: Use isosceles triangles formed by radii and tangents.
answer_outline: Join external point P to center O

questions.question: Prove that if a tangent and a secant are drawn from an external point, the square of the tangent equals the product of external and whole secant segments.
questions.answer: Use triangles formed by tangent, secant, and radii to establish equality.
answer_outline: Draw tangent PT and secant PAB from external point P
Use similarity or power of point theorem

questions.question: Prove that if two tangents meet at an external point, the external angle formed is supplementary to the angle subtended at the center by the line joining points of contact.
questions.answer: Use triangle formed with center and tangent points to show angle relation.
answer_outline: Join center O to points of contact A and B

questions.question: Prove that the angle formed by two intersecting chords is equal to half the sum of angles subtended by the chords at the circumference.
questions.answer: Use properties of intersecting chords and cyclic quadrilaterals to derive formula.
answer_outline: Draw intersecting chords AB and CD at P

questions.question: Prove that if two tangents intersect at an external point, the angle between them equals the difference of the angles subtended at the center by lines joining the points of contact.
questions.answer: Construct triangle using center and points of contact and use central angle property.
answer_outline: Join center to points of contact
Conclude angle between tangents = difference of central angles

questions.question: Prove that if a secant and a tangent intersect at an external point, the angle between them equals half the difference of angles subtended by the secant at the circumference.
questions.answer: Use properties of intersecting lines and angles subtended by secant.
answer_outline: Draw tangent PT and secant PAB

questions.question: Prove that the sum of the squares of the lengths of tangents from an external point to two circles equals the square of the distance between centers if circles are touching externally.
questions.answer: Use power of a point theorem and touching circle property to show equality.
answer_outline: Draw externally touching circles with centers O1 and O2
Draw tangents from external point P
Conclude sum of squares = distance² between centers

questions.question: Prove that the center of a circle lies on the bisector of an angle formed by two intersecting chords.
questions.answer: Use triangle formed by chord intersection and radius to show center lies on angle bisector.
answer_outline: Draw intersecting chords AB and CD

questions.question: Prove that the line joining centers of two internally touching circles passes through the point of contact.
questions.answer: Distance between centers equals difference of radii, so line joining centers passes through contact point.
answer_outline: Mark centers and point of contact
Conclude line passes through contact point

questions.question: Prove that the lengths of tangents from an external point to a circle are equal using Pythagoras theorem.
questions.answer: Construct right triangles with radius and tangent, apply Pythagoras theorem to show equality of tangent lengths.
answer_outline: Draw external point P and tangents PA and PB
Draw radii to points of contact
